title: hibiscus barbadensis var. klotzschianus
intro: Hibiscus barbadensis var. klotzschianus, also known as Hibiscus sabdariffa, is a plant species within the mallow family (Malvaceae) that is commonly referred to as the roselle or Jamaica sorrel. The plant's calyces, which are the fleshy, sepals of the flower, have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including antidiabetic, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects. These properties contribute to various health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels, improving digestion, and reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. Hibiscus sabdariffa is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed as a beverage or used topically to address various health concerns.
